denial_pattern_sources: incorrect_demographic_guideline: Commonwealth Fund 2023 Review of Prior Auth Denials
algo_reviewer_no_credentials: ERISA 29 U.S.C. § 1133; MHPAEA Final Rule 2024 (29 CFR § 2590.712); Hansens v. Cigna (D. Conn. 2023)
denial_letter_text:
Dear Plan Member,

We received your provider's request on 04/14/2026 for Psychiatric partial hospitalization related to Panic disorder with agoraphobia (F40.01). After review, we cannot approve this request.

Our review applied Aetna Clinical Policy Bulletin (CPB) criteria and InterQual review standards. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Psychiatric partial hospitalization is non-preferred; a formulary alternative must be tried for Panic disorder with agoraphobia (F40.01). A lower-tier agent is available without demonstrated failure of the preferred option.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with formulary tier dispute. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. The requesting provider may submit additional records, but the current file does not support approval.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

You have the right to appeal this determination. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this letter. Include your member identification number and the service reference shown on your explanation of benefits.

Sincerely,

Aetna Medical Management
clinical_context: The 67-year-old member has Panic disorder with agoraphobia (F40.01). She has been treated under commercial coverage with Psychiatric partial hospitalization recommended as the next appropriate step. Multiple ER visits in 2025; lorazepam taper completed; SSRIs partially effective only. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that formulary tier dispute bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings that were submitted with the original request but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if her care is delayed further.

insurer_voice.reasoning: Letter reads as authentic Aetna correspondence with CPB and InterQual fingerprints and procedural tone.
